code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-MAR-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal dysfunction and/or tubular damage. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Further nephrological evaluation and clinical correlation are recommended.
